symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly worsening bilateral peripheral pitting oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Volume overload from renal sodium/water retention and hypoalbuminaemia produces symmetrical pitting oedema.
Bilateral symmetrical swelling is against a unilateral venous thrombosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: New exertional dyspnoea relieved by rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Diabetic nephropathy with fluid overload / evolving pulmonary oedema
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Breathlessness signals fluid moving into the lungs — a red flag for decompensation.
Exertional dyspnoea is common to cardiac and renal volume overload; distinguishing requires investigation.
Visible urinary frothing reflects heavy proteinuria, characteristic of a protein-losing glomerular (renal) pathology.
Poorly controlled hypertension both drives and reflects renal injury and volume overload; patient-obtainable via home cuff, unverified.
Severe elevation with target-organ strain raises hypertensive emergency risk.
Nocturia from osmotic diuresis and progressive renal impairment; non-specific but consistent.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea and profound anorexia limiting oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anorexia and nausea can reflect advancing renal dysfunction; needs biochemical confirmation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bibasal fine inspiratory crepitations on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bibasal crepitations indicate pulmonary congestion — a clinician auscultation finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: 3+ pitting oedema bilaterally to the mid-calves (clinician-graded)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Formal grading and extent of pitting oedema is a clinician examination finding — sealed, not remotely obtainable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: BP 185/95, HR 92, RR 22, SpO2 94%, Temp 36.8C

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Diabetic nephropathy with fluid overload / evolving pulmonary oedema
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachypnoea and hypoxia with severe hypertension objectively corroborate decompensation; clinician-measured, sealed. The AI Doctor infers concern from reported breathlessness and home BP, not from these measured values.

symptoms.symptom_name: Persistently elevated urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio with declining eGFR

investigation_details.investigation_name: Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio and serum creatinine/eGFR
investigation_details.expected_result: Persistently elevated urine ACR with a slowly declining eGFR
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without quantified proteinuria and eGFR the severity of renal impairment cannot be staged or safely managed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated ACR with declining eGFR in a long-standing diabetic is the diagnostic hallmark of diabetic nephropathy — requires laboratory investigation, sealed.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Nephrotic-range fluid overload with respiratory decompensation
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Diabetic nephropathy with fluid overload / evolving pulmonary oedema
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Frothy urine + bilateral oedema + high BP + new exertional dyspnoea together indicate a protein-losing renal pathology decompensating into volume overload and early pulmonary oedema.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Stages renal impairment and detects AKI-on-CKD, hyperkalaemia and the safety of any diuretic/RAAS therapy — obtainable only in person.
Quantifies proteinuria underlying the frothy urine and supports the diabetic-nephropathy diagnosis.
Assesses degree of pulmonary oedema in a patient with new dyspnoea, crepitations and hypoxia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine renal ultrasound in the acute telehealth encounter
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not the immediate priority over urgent bloods, chest assessment and in-person review; ordering it should not delay escalation.
